theme: First Dutch Agency Focused on AI for Communications
headline: Het eerste bureau in Nederland dat zich specifiek richt op AI-oplossingen voor communicatieprofessionals
ghostStatement: AI4Comms is the first agency in the Netherlands specifically focused on AI solutions for communications professionals. This is not a generic AI consultancy that added a communications module, nor a communications agency that bolted on an AI product. AI4Comms was built from the start around the specific workflows, governance needs, and stakeholder pressures of the communications function.

headline: AI versterkt het communicatieteam — mensen blijven aan het stuur
ghostStatement: Where others sell AI as disruption or efficiency that replaces headcount, AI4Comms is built on a different conviction: AI is a partner that accelerates the communications function, with humans in charge of strategy, judgment, and relationships. Every service is designed around that principle — from how we train teams to how we design governance frameworks.

headline: AI4Comms slaat de brug tussen corporate communicatie-expertise en state-of-the-art AI-toepassingen
ghostStatement: AI4Comms was founded by communications professionals who understand the actual work of corporate communications, public affairs, and media relations — not technologists explaining AI to communicators. That difference matters: advice is grounded in how communications teams actually operate, what governance risks are real, and which AI applications create genuine value versus noise.

theme: Governance and Responsible AI from the Start
headline: AI-governance voor de communicatiefunctie — reputatierisico en compliance ingebouwd, niet achteraf toegevoegd
ghostStatement: Most AI adoption in communications happens first and governs later — creating reputational, legal, and workflow risk that is expensive to fix. AI4Comms builds governance into every engagement from the start: risk registers, usage policies, and approval workflows that are calibrated to the communications function's specific exposure, not borrowed from generic IT frameworks.
